articles.title: Cisco Releases Security Updates for Actively Exploited SD-WAN Manager Flaw
articles.summary: Cisco has released security updates for a medium-severity security flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out of 10.0. "A vulnerability in the web UI of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manag

articles.title: CISA Flags LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaw Exploited for Root Privilege Escalation
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a security flaw impacting LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 18, 2026. The vulnerability in que

articles.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
articles.summary: A vulnerability in the SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protocol. [...]

articles.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
articles.summary: A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email. The way in was a backdoor on their REDCap research servers that stole login credentials. The exfiltration was the un

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two malicious cyber campaigns that exhibit similarities with a persistent North Korean threat cluster known as Contagious Interview (aka Famous Chollima, HexagonalRodent, and Void Dokkaebi). According to a report published by Proofpoint, the threat actor has be

articles.title: Maine closes data breach portal to the public after fake reports
articles.summary: Maine is still allowing companies to report breaches, but won’t make the portal easily available to the public until after it completes an audit of its procedures to stop such incidents, according to a press release from the Maine attorney general’s office.

articles.title: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hacked in CDN supply-chain attack
articles.summary: WordPress plugins OptinMonster, TrustPulse, and PushEngage have been compromised in a supply-chain attack impacting Awesome Motive-s content distribution network (CDN). [...]

articles.title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
articles.summary: Cisco has released security updates to address a vulnerability in the Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, that was exploited in attacks to escalate to root privileges. [...]

articles.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
articles.summary: A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed LiteLLM is a widely deployed open-source AI gateway that brokers calls to more than 100 model providers behind one OpenA

articles.title: Council of Europe investigates ShinyHunters data breach claims
articles.summary: The Council of Europe, the continent's oldest intergovernmental body, is probing claims of a data breach made by the ShinyHunters extortion group over the weekend. [...]

articles.title: Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer
articles.summary: Mackay Sugar was targeted in a cyberattack carried out by a threat group known as The Gentlemen. The post Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
articles.summary: A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search. Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into a one-click exfiltration path they call SearchLeak. Because the link point

articles.title: Cyberattack on Russian tech firm Astral disrupts business, government services for week
articles.summary: According to customer complaints, the disruption affected a range of services used by businesses, leading to interruptions in cash register operations, difficulties selling certain regulated goods, loss of access to customer portals and corporate email and problems with electronic human resources do

articles.title: Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America
articles.summary: Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has been tracking the cyberespionage group as UNC6508 since early 2025. The post Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research
articles.summary: A China-linked espionage campaign targeted exposed REDCap servers to deploy the InfiniteRed malware and steal sensitive data from a medical institution in North America. [...]

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Day, UniFi Exploits, macOS Stealers, VPN Flaw and More
articles.summary: Stuff broke again. Not in a movie way. An old tool was left exposed. An abandoned package was abused. A deprecated feature was still running in prod. This week is the same lesson in a new form: phishing kits are easier to rent, AI names are useful bait, old login paths still fail, and forgotten soft

articles.title: New attack turned Microsoft 365 Copilot into 1-click data theft tool
articles.summary: A critical vulnerability chain dubbed SearchLeak in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise could allow attackers to steal sensitive data from a target's mailbox, OneDrive, or SharePoint account through a specially crafted URL. [...]

articles.title: Infinite Campus data breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts
articles.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from more than 137,000 school staff accounts in a Salesforce data theft attack that targeted the widely used Infinite Campus K-12 student information system in March. [...]

articles.title: Webinar: How behavioral AI stops phishing and account takeovers
articles.summary: Modern phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks increasingly bypass traditional email defenses and create operational strain for security teams. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection, investigation, and remediation to reduce alert fatigue and accelerate response time

articles.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
articles.summary: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. articles.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
articles.summary: Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang. The post Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
articles.summary: The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. The post Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker
articles.summary: French officials say roughly 73,000 government accounts were affected, while the threat actor claims to have stolen messages and user data from the sovereign Tchap platform. The post French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack
articles.summary: The extortion group threatens to leak 297 GB of data allegedly stolen from the Council of Europe, including employee personal information. The post ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
articles.summary: An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites. When a site administrator was logged in as the file loaded, the code created an admin account under the attacker's cont

articles.title: Maine Disables Data Breach Portal Due to Fake Submissions
articles.summary: Someone posted fake VRChat and Discord data breach reports on the system, prompting the Maine AG to take action. The post Maine Disables Data Breach Portal Due to Fake Submissions appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Evil MSI Background: BASE64 Statistical Analysis, (Mon, Jun 15th)
articles.summary: I like it when a fellow handler posts a diary entry about images with malicious content. Last one is Xavier: "The Evil MSI Background is Back!".

articles.title: Palo Alto Warns of Active Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Flaw
articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks has revealed that it has observed "active exploitation" of a recently disclosed PAN-OS vulnerability by an unknown threat actor to obtain unauthorized access to GlobalProtect portals. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw a

articles.title: Belarus-linked hackers target Gmail accounts of Polish public figures and their families
articles.summary: Poland has warned that Ghostwriter, the Belarus-linked hacker group, has expanded its phishing operations to target personal Gmail accounts belonging to senior public figures and their relatives.

articles.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
articles.summary: A former IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison after conducting a prolonged cyberattack against the former employer that disrupted classroom operations, deleted accounts, and caused tens of thousands of dollars in damages. [...]

articles.title: NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks
articles.summary: By default, npm install will no longer execute scripts from dependencies, unless explicitly allowed. The post NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
articles.summary: Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system. "In Splunk Enterpris

articles.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
articles.summary: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the future. [...]

articles.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
articles.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. articles.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
articles.summary: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans. articles.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
articles.summary: A 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the phpBB forum software allows an attacker to log in as any user, including administrators. [...]

articles.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
articles.summary: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. articles.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
articles.summary: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]

articles.title: Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
articles.summary: About 7 million customers of the genetics testing company had their data stolen by hackers starting in April 2023, and many had their information posted on the dark web.

articles.title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
articles.summary: More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]

articles.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
articles.summary: The penalty is the largest ever issued by the commission for a personal data breach, surpassing the record 134.8 billion won ($88.8 million) fine levied against SK Telecom earlier this year.

articles.title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
articles.summary: GitHub access sales, leaked repositories, and stolen API keys can all become supply-chain attack footholds. Flare explores how underground forums expose early signals tied to software supply-chain risk. [...]

articles.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines. articles.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack
articles.summary: The hackers published 5GB of data, including customer personal information and credentials for the RTKBase platform. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, the world's largest producer of insulin, disclosed a data breach affecting patient information from some clinical trials. [...]
